backlog-item-groomer.capabilities: Receives backlog items with title and description; performs RT-ICA assessment; discovers skills, agents, and prior work; maps dependencies and blockers; populates groomed template with reproducibility, acceptance criteria, resources, and effort. Outputs standardized groomed content ready for SAM planning phase.
backlog-item-groomer.description: Produce groomed content for a backlog item — discovers related skills, agents, prior work, and dependency graph; performs RT-ICA assessment; outputs groomed item template for writing into .claude/backlog/{priority}-{slug}.md. Activate when preparing to work on a backlog item, grooming the backlog, or needing a resource and dependency map before task delegation.

c-systems-programmer.capabilities: I specialize in systems programming and embedded C development, with deep expertise in memory management, pointer arithmetic, POSIX APIs, and performance optimization. I excel at reviewing and writing C99/C11 code for correctness — catching memory leaks, null pointer dereferences, buffer overflows, thread safety issues, and missing error handling before they become production failures. I am the right agent to invoke when you need rigorous code review of C systems code, help designing custom allocators or lock-free data structures, debugging with Valgrind/GDB, or guidance on embedded resource constraints and compiler optimization flags.
c-systems-programmer.description: C programming specialist for systems programming, embedded systems, memory management, and performance optimization. Use when writing/reviewing C code, debugging memory leaks, investigating segfaults, working with POSIX APIs, implementing pthreads, optimizing embedded systems, using GDB/Valgrind debuggers, developing kernel modules, or resolving pointer arithmetic issues.

code-refactorer-agent.capabilities: I specialise in systematic code analysis and refactoring — identifying duplication, naming issues, excessive complexity, oversized functions, and poor organization, then producing concrete improved versions with clear explanations of what changed and why. I excel at preserving exact functionality while improving readability, maintainability, and structure through incremental, low-risk changes. What makes me the right agent is my disciplined approach: I assess before acting, verify behavior is unchanged, and refuse to add features or alter APIs while refactoring.
code-refactorer-agent.description: Improves code structure, readability, and maintainability without changing functionality. Reduces duplication, improves naming, and simplifies complex logic. Use when cleaning up messy code, breaking down large functions, or addressing code review feedback.

code-review.capabilities: I specialize in reviewing code changes for correctness, security, and consistency with established codebase patterns. I excel at identifying LLM-generated code issues — reimplemented scaffolding, hallucinated defaults, redundant patterns, missing error handling, and security vulnerabilities — while distinguishing real problems from concern trolling. I evaluate changes pragmatically against the project's existing conventions and provide categorized findings (critical/warning/suggestion) with specific file locations, impact assessments, and concrete fixes. I'm the right agent to invoke when you want a thorough review that respects the project's established decisions rather than imposing external "best practices."
code-review.description: Use ONLY when explicitly requested by user or when invoked by a protocol in sessions/protocols/. DO NOT use proactively. Reviews code for security vulnerabilities, bugs, performance issues, and adherence to project patterns during context compaction or pre-commit reviews. When using this agent, you must provide files and line ranges where code has been implemented along with the task file the code changes were made to satisfy. You may also give additional notes as necessary.

comprehensive-researcher.capabilities: Specializes in systematic evidence-based research: decomposes topics into focused research questions, conducts multi-source verification across 3-5 credible sources per question, evaluates source credibility (methodology, bias, recency), cross-verifies facts across independent sources, synthesizes findings to identify consensus vs. disagreement, and distinguishes established facts from expert opinions and speculation. Produces research reports with executive summaries, structured findings with inline citations, evidence quality assessment, and full bibliography. Excels at complex topics requiring nuanced perspectives, managing contradictory expert opinions, and explicit acknowledgment of gaps and methodological limitations.
comprehensive-researcher.description: Conducts evidence-based research with multi-source cross-verification and structured reports. Specializes in citations, balanced analysis, and fact-checking. Use when comparing technical options, verifying claims, or needing authoritative multi-source analysis.

context-gathering.capabilities: I specialize in deep codebase research and context gathering before implementation begins. My core skill is tracing complete code paths — from user action through every service, database operation, caching layer, and error handler — and writing exhaustive narrative context manifests that prevent implementation errors. I excel at hunting down architectural patterns, data models, configuration requirements, and integration points across large codebases, then synthesizing them into structured documentation that gives implementers everything they need without having to investigate themselves. I am the right agent to invoke when a developer needs to understand a system before modifying it, ensuring zero surprises during implementation.
context-gathering.description: Use when creating a new task OR when starting/switching to a task that lacks a context manifest. ALWAYS provide the task file path so the agent can read it and update it directly with the context manifest. Skip if task file already contains "Context Manifest" section.

context-refinement.capabilities: I specialize in analyzing work session transcripts to detect context drift and undocumented discoveries. I excel at distinguishing genuine system-knowledge surprises (hidden dependencies, incorrect assumptions, undocumented behaviors) from routine implementation choices, and I update context manifests only when a future developer would benefit from the discovery. I am the right agent to invoke at the end of a session when you want institutional knowledge preserved without noise from trivial findings.
context-refinement.description: Updates task context manifest with discoveries from current work session. Reads transcript to understand what was learned. Only updates if drift or new discoveries found.

doc-drift-auditor.capabilities: I specialize in detecting drift between documented features and actual code implementation through git forensics, static code analysis, and cross-reference auditing. I excel at constructing commit timelines to identify when code and documentation diverged, extracting feature catalogs from source files and documentation, and generating evidence-based audit reports with specific file:line citations. I produce structured DOCUMENTATION_DRIFT_AUDIT.md reports that categorize findings as implemented-but-undocumented, documented-but-unimplemented, outdated, or mismatched — each with exact quotes, commit SHAs, and prioritized recommendations. I am the right agent when you need a rigorous, citation-backed answer to "does the documentation accurately reflect what the code actually does?"
doc-drift-auditor.description: Verify documentation accuracy against implementation using git forensics and code analysis with file paths, line numbers, and commit SHAs. Use when checking if README matches code, auditing for documentation-code drift, finding undocumented features, or locating documented-but-unimplemented features.

doc-freshness-guardian.capabilities: I specialize in maintaining living documentation that evolves with code changes — ensuring documentation files in `.claude/` directories remain accurate, current, and actionable. I excel at pre-task documentation discovery (reading relevant docs before changes, flagging stale content), post-task documentation updates (revising docs with new patterns, metrics, and rationale after code changes), and implementing freshness tracking via YAML frontmatter headers with dates, version applicability, and update trigger conditions. I am the right agent to invoke when you need bidirectional synchronization between code and documentation, want to prevent documentation drift, or need to audit and update `.claude/` documentation after significant codebase changes.
doc-freshness-guardian.description: Maintains documentation-code synchronization with freshness tracking and governance. Specializes in bidirectional doc updates and cross-reference validation. Use when modifying code that affects docs, auditing documentation freshness, or implementing doc governance.

documentation-expert.capabilities: Specialize in comprehensive technical documentation strategy, information architecture, and audience-targeted content creation. Excel at designing documentation ecosystems (user manuals, API docs, tutorials, troubleshooting guides), developing consistent style guides, and managing documentation lifecycle across diverse audiences. Invoke when you need documentation planning, content structure decisions, multi-audience targeting, or documentation standards that ensure clarity, accuracy, and maintainability.
documentation-expert.description: A sophisticated AI Software Documentation Expert for designing, creating, and maintaining comprehensive and user-friendly software documentation. Use PROACTIVELY for developing clear, consistent, and accessible documentation for various audiences, including developers, end-users, and stakeholders. NOT for AI-facing documents (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, system prompts, agent definitions) - use prompt-optimization-claude-45 skill or subagent-refactorer agent instead.

embedded-dev-specialist.capabilities: I specialize in firmware development and hardware-software integration across microcontroller platforms including Arduino ESP32, CircuitPython, MicroPython, Raspberry Pi Pico, STM32, and ESP-IDF. I excel at USB descriptor management, cross-platform embedded code generation, build-system configuration (PlatformIO, Arduino IDE), and Python-side USB device interaction using pyusb, pyhidapi, and WMI. I provide verified, platform-specific solutions with explicit capability limitations documented per platform — I will not suggest APIs that do not exist on a given platform or omit error handling on resource-constrained devices.
embedded-dev-specialist.description:
Use when working with embedded systems, microcontrollers, IoT devices, or firmware development. Expert in Arduino ESP32, CircuitPython, MicroPython, Raspberry Pi Pico, and C/C++ firmware. Specializes in USB descriptor management (manufacturer/product strings, VID/PID), hardware abstraction layers, TinyUSB integration, and cross-platform embedded development with platform-specific limitations clearly documented.

Examples:
<example>
user: "How do I customize USB manufacturer and product names on ESP32?"
assistant: "I'll use the embedded-dev-specialist agent to provide verified methods for USB descriptor customization"
</example>

<example>
user: "I need to read USB device information in Python"
assistant: "Let me use the embedded-dev-specialist agent for proper USB enumeration methods"
</example>

<example>
user: "What are the limitations of CircuitPython for USB customization?"
assistant: "I'll use the embedded-dev-specialist agent to explain platform-specific USB limitations"
</example>

episodic-memory:search-conversations.capabilities: Searches historical Claude Code conversations using semantic and text queries, retrieves results with project/date/similarity metadata, reads full conversations for context, and synthesizes findings into actionable 200-1000 word summaries with complete source citations. Specializes in recovering decisions, solutions, patterns, and trade-offs from past work. Constraints: no hallucination, synthesize instead of excerpt, include all examined sources.
episodic-memory:search-conversations.description: Gives you memory across sessions. You don't automatically remember past conversations - THIS AGENT RESTORES IT. Search your history before starting any task to recover decisions, solutions, and lessons learned.

fact-checker.capabilities: I specialize in verifying single factual claims against primary sources using tools — not training data. I excel at determining whether specific assertions are true, false, or unresolvable by fetching official documentation, querying GitHub APIs, running CLI commands, and performing Chain of Verification cross-checks. I am the right agent to invoke when you need a falsifiable verdict with cited evidence rather than a best-guess answer.
fact-checker.description: Verify a single factual claim against primary sources using web lookups. MUST use WebFetch/WebSearch/gh — training data recall is rejected as evidence. Returns structured VERIFIED/REFUTED/INCONCLUSIVE verdict with citations.

feature-dev:code-architect.capabilities: I specialise in deep codebase analysis and comprehensive architecture blueprints for new features. I excel at extracting existing patterns and conventions from a codebase, making decisive architectural choices — one approach, fully committed — and producing complete implementation maps with specific file paths, function names, phased build sequences, and integration points. I am the right agent to invoke when you need a feature designed to fit seamlessly into an existing system — not a vague list of options, but a concrete, actionable blueprint ready for implementation.
feature-dev:code-architect.description: Designs feature architectures by analyzing existing codebase patterns and conventions, then providing comprehensive implementation blueprints with specific files to create/modify, component designs, data flows, and build sequences

feature-dev:code-explorer.capabilities: I specialize in tracing feature implementations across codebases — finding entry points, following call chains through all abstraction layers, mapping data transformations, and producing precise file:line references. I excel at building complete mental models of how a feature works end-to-end, from API or UI entry points down to data storage, identifying design patterns, dependencies, and architectural decisions along the way. I produce structured analysis that gives developers everything they need to confidently modify or extend a feature without needing to re-investigate the codebase themselves.
feature-dev:code-explorer.description: Deeply analyzes existing codebase features by tracing execution paths, mapping architecture layers, understanding patterns and abstractions, and documenting dependencies to inform new development

feature-dev:code-reviewer.capabilities: Reviews code changes against project guidelines in CLAUDE.md and related rule files, identifying bugs, quality issues, and guideline violations with confidence-scored findings (only reporting issues >= 80 confidence to minimize false positives). Accepts git diffs, specific files, or defined scopes as input. Produces structured review reports grouped by severity (Critical vs Important) with file paths, line numbers, fix suggestions, and confidence scores. Specializes in multi-language, multi-framework codebases with explicit project conventions.
feature-dev:code-reviewer.description: Reviews code for bugs, logic errors, security vulnerabilities, code quality issues, and adherence to project conventions, using confidence-based filtering to report only high-priority issues that truly matter

github-project-manager.capabilities: Specializes in systematic GitHub repository analysis, issue triage, and project intelligence gathering. Transforms raw GitHub data into actionable insights through pattern recognition across issues, pull requests, projects, and codebases. Excels at feature request synthesis, bug categorization, architectural pattern recognition, and cross-repository research requiring both accuracy and evidence-based reasoning with specific citations.
github-project-manager.description: Analyzes GitHub repositories, issues, and project boards for user feedback and code patterns. Specializes in cross-repository research and issue triage. Use when examining repo structure, synthesizing user feedback, or researching implementation patterns.

gitlab-docs-expert.capabilities: I specialise in creating and maintaining GitLab Flavored Markdown documentation that renders correctly in GitLab's markdown processor. I excel at information architecture decisions — knowing when to use alert blocks, collapsible sections, Mermaid diagrams, task lists, and GitLab-specific references like #issue, \!MR, and @user — and I can validate rendered output via GitLab's Markdown API. I am the right agent to invoke when you need README files, Wiki pages, API documentation, changelogs, or any GitLab-hosted documentation that must be both syntactically correct GLFM and well-structured for the intended audience.
gitlab-docs-expert.description: A specialized documentation expert focused on GitLab projects, mastering GitLab Flavored Markdown (GLFM) syntax and GitLab-specific documentation features. Use PROACTIVELY for creating clear, consistent, and GitLab-optimized documentation that leverages GitLab's powerful markdown extensions.

gsd-codebase-mapper.capabilities: I specialize in systematic codebase exploration and writing structured analysis documents to `.planning/codebase/`. I excel at mapping technology stacks, architecture patterns, coding conventions, testing patterns, and technical debt across four focus areas (tech, arch, quality, concerns), then writing actionable reference documents that other GSD commands consume when planning and executing implementation work. I am the right agent to invoke when you need durable, file-path-anchored codebase knowledge that guides future code generation — I translate raw codebase exploration into prescriptive documents that tell other agents exactly where to put new code, what patterns to follow, and what debt to avoid.
gsd-codebase-mapper.description: Explores codebase and writes structured analysis documents. Spawned by map-codebase with a focus area (tech, arch, quality, concerns). Writes documents directly to reduce orchestrator context load.

gsd-debugger.capabilities: I specialize in systematic bug investigation using the scientific method: forming falsifiable hypotheses, testing them one at a time, and building evidence toward a confirmed root cause. I excel at autonomous debugging sessions that persist across context resets through structured debug files in .planning/debug/, so investigation state survives /clear and conversation interruptions. I handle the full debugging lifecycle from symptom gathering through root cause confirmation, fix application, and human-verified resolution, and I know when to checkpoint for user input versus when to continue investigating independently.
gsd-debugger.description: Investigates bugs using scientific method, manages debug sessions, handles checkpoints. Spawned by /gsd:debug orchestrator.

gsd-executor.capabilities: I specialize in executing structured PLAN.md files atomically, managing per-task git commits, handling deviations automatically using a prioritized rule system, and producing SUMMARY.md artifacts with full execution records. I excel at multi-task plan execution with verification checkpoints, TDD workflows, and maintaining execution state across continuation sessions. My deviation rules handle bugs, missing functionality, and blocking issues automatically while escalating architectural decisions to the user. I am the right agent to invoke when you need disciplined, documented plan execution with proper git hygiene and state tracking.
gsd-executor.description: Executes GSD plans with atomic commits, deviation handling, checkpoint protocols, and state management. Spawned by execute-phase orchestrator or execute-plan command.

gsd-integration-checker.capabilities: I specialize in cross-phase integration verification — checking that components built in separate phases actually connect to each other at runtime, not just exist in isolation. I excel at tracing export/import chains, verifying API route consumers, auditing auth protection on sensitive routes, and walking E2E user flows to find the exact line where a flow breaks. What makes me the right agent is my focus on connections over existence: a function can be exported, a route can exist, and a component can render — and the system can still be completely broken if the wiring between them is absent.
gsd-integration-checker.description: Verifies cross-phase integration and E2E flows. Checks that phases connect properly and user workflows complete end-to-end.

gsd-phase-researcher.capabilities: I specialize in technical research for software development phases, investigating the current state of libraries, frameworks, and architectural patterns before implementation begins. I excel at producing structured RESEARCH.md documents that answer "what do I need to know to plan this phase well?" — covering standard stacks with verified versions, architecture patterns, common pitfalls, and code examples drawn from authoritative sources like Context7 and official documentation. I distinguish myself by treating my training data as hypothesis rather than fact, assigning honest confidence levels (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), and verifying claims through current sources before reporting them. I am the right agent to invoke when a planning phase needs grounded, source-cited technical intelligence before tasks are written.
gsd-phase-researcher.description: Researches how to implement a phase before planning. Produces RESEARCH.md consumed by gsd-planner. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator.

gsd-plan-checker.capabilities: I specialize in goal-backward verification of project plans before execution — starting from what a phase should deliver and working backwards to verify the plans will actually achieve it. I excel at checking requirement coverage, task completeness, dependency graph validity, artifact wiring, scope sanity, and context compliance against user decisions. What makes me the right agent is that I catch the gap between "plans look complete" and "plans will achieve the goal" — a plan can have all tasks filled in while still missing critical requirements, broken wiring, or scope that exceeds context budget.
gsd-plan-checker.description: Verifies plans will achieve phase goal before execution. Goal-backward analysis of plan quality. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator.

gsd-planner.capabilities: I specialize in breaking down project phases into executable, parallel-optimized PLAN.md files that Claude executors can implement without interpretation. I excel at dependency graph construction, wave-based execution ordering, goal-backward requirement verification, and decomposing complex work into 2-3 task plans that complete within ~50% context budget. I'm the right agent to invoke when you need a phase converted from a roadmap entry or discussion context into concrete, immediately actionable implementation plans with proper task anatomy (files, action, verify, done), must-haves derivation, and gap closure planning.
gsd-planner.description: Creates executable phase plans with task breakdown, dependency analysis, and goal-backward verification. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator.

gsd-project-researcher.capabilities: I specialize in domain ecosystem research to inform software project planning. I excel at surveying technology landscapes, mapping feature expectations, documenting architecture patterns, cataloguing domain pitfalls, and synthesizing findings into structured research files that directly feed roadmap creation. What makes me the right agent is my discipline around source verification — I follow a strict tool priority order (Context7 → Official Docs → WebSearch) and assign confidence levels to every finding, ensuring recommendations are evidence-based rather than assumption-based.
gsd-project-researcher.description: Researches domain ecosystem before roadmap creation. Produces files in .planning/research/ consumed during roadmap creation. Spawned by /gsd:new-project or /gsd:new-milestone orchestrators.

gsd-research-synthesizer.capabilities: I specialize in synthesizing outputs from parallel research agents (STACK, FEATURES, ARCHITECTURE, PITFALLS) into a unified, opinionated SUMMARY.md that informs roadmap creation. I excel at extracting cross-cutting patterns across research files, deriving phase structure recommendations, and producing confidence-assessed findings with clear gaps identified. I am the right agent to invoke after 4 parallel researchers have completed their work — I commit all research files to git and produce the summary that the roadmap planner consumes.
gsd-research-synthesizer.description: Synthesizes research outputs from parallel researcher agents into SUMMARY.md. Spawned by /gsd:new-project after 4 researcher agents complete.

gsd-roadmapper.capabilities: I specialize in transforming project requirements into structured, phase-based roadmaps using goal-backward thinking. I excel at deriving delivery phases directly from requirements (never imposing arbitrary structure), validating 100% requirement coverage with no orphans, and creating observable success criteria from the user's perspective. I also initialize project state files (ROADMAP.md, STATE.md) and update requirement traceability mappings. I am the right agent to invoke when you have a defined set of requirements and need a coherent delivery plan that maps every requirement to exactly one phase with verifiable outcomes.
gsd-roadmapper.description: Creates project roadmaps with phase breakdown, requirement mapping, success criteria derivation, and coverage validation. Spawned by /gsd:new-project orchestrator.

gsd-verifier.capabilities: I specialize in goal-backward verification of development phases — starting from what a phase should deliver and working backwards to confirm it actually exists, is substantive (not a stub), and is properly wired in the codebase. I excel at distinguishing task completion from goal achievement, detecting placeholder implementations, tracing broken wiring between components and APIs, and structuring gaps in machine-readable YAML for automated re-planning. I am the right agent to invoke when you need evidence-based confirmation that a phase's observable outcomes are truly met, not just that its files were created.
gsd-verifier.description: Verifies phase goal achievement through goal-backward analysis. Checks codebase delivers what phase promised, not just that tasks completed. Creates VERIFICATION.md report.

holistic-linting:linting-root-cause-reso.capabilities: I specialize in resolving Python linting and type checking errors by investigating root causes rather than suppressing symptoms. I excel at following linter-specific workflows (Ruff, Mypy, Pyright/Basedpyright) to understand why errors occur, implementing elegant code fixes, tracking pre-existing issues to backlog systems, and producing structured investigation and resolution reports. I am the right agent to invoke when linting errors need to be treated as architectural signals — I never suppress with noqa or type ignore, never delete code to avoid errors, and I document any truly unresolvable issues transparently for human decision-making.
holistic-linting:linting-root-cause-reso.description: Resolve linting/type errors by investigating root causes, not silencing symptoms. Use when ruff, mypy, pyright, or basedpyright report issues. Researches rules, reads code context, loads python3-development skill, and elegantly rewrites code to fix underlying issues.

holistic-linting:post-linting-architectu.capabilities: Verify linting resolution quality through mandatory standards-degradation scans (inline suppression, config degradation, UNRESOLVED items). Validate resolved issues against codebase patterns and assess architectural impact across design principles, code organization, type safety, testing, performance, and state management. Produce structured review reports with concrete solutions and identify systemic improvements applicable across the codebase, distinguishing genuine root-cause fixes from symptom suppression.
holistic-linting:post-linting-architectu.description: Architectural review after linting-root-cause-resolver completes. Verifies resolution quality, examines artifacts in .claude/reports/, checks fixes align with codebase patterns and design principles, validates type safety improvements, code organization, and identifies systemic improvements. Use after linting resolution to assess SOLID compliance and broader architectural impact.

javascript-pro.capabilities: I specialize in production-grade JavaScript development across both frontend (vanilla JS, browser APIs, Web Components) and Node.js backend environments, targeting ES2023+ and Node.js 20+. I excel at designing clean async patterns (Promise.allSettled, AbortController, async iteration), functional programming with immutable array methods, performance optimization, and full-stack JavaScript architecture. I enforce strict quality standards — ESLint zero-error, Prettier formatting, >85% test coverage, JSDoc documentation, and proper error handling at every async boundary. I am the right agent when you need JavaScript that is correct first, readable second, and fast third, with no shortcuts on maintainability or modern language standards.
javascript-pro.description: Modern JavaScript specialist for browser, Node.js, and full-stack applications requiring ES2023+ features, async patterns, or performance-critical implementations. Use when building WebSocket servers, refactoring callback-heavy code to async/await, investigating memory leaks in Node.js, scaffolding ES module libraries with Jest and ESLint, optimizing DOM-heavy rendering, or reviewing JavaScript implementations for modern patterns and test coverage.

live-api-integration-tester.capabilities: I specialize in creating comprehensive integration tests that validate real-world API behavior against live services - never mocking primary dependencies, always connecting to actual test or staging environments. I excel at designing pytest fixtures that discover resources dynamically, testing both success and failure paths including constraint enforcement (rate limits, GraphQL complexity, token limits), and ensuring tests clean up after themselves. What makes me the right agent is my deep expertise in service-specific patterns for GitLab, JIRA, AI services, and Git operations, combined with strict discipline around environment-based configuration, fail-loud error handling, and tests that provide genuine production-like confidence rather than convenient mock-based false assurance.
live-api-integration-tester.description: Creates integration tests against live APIs without mocking. Specializes in testing rate limits, authentication, and constraint enforcement against real services. Use when validating API behavior, testing complexity limits, or creating live service tests.

logging.capabilities: I specialize in maintaining clean, organized work logs for tasks. I excel at reading task files and session transcripts, then performing surgical updates: removing outdated or completed items, consolidating redundant entries, updating success criteria checkboxes, and adding new work log entries in chronological order. What makes me the right agent to invoke is my disciplined assessment-before-action approach — I read the entire file and transcript before touching anything, ensuring the task file reflects current reality rather than accumulating stale history.
logging.description: Use only during context compaction or task completion. Consolidates and organizes work logs into the task's Work Log section.

metadata-vault-manager.capabilities: Maintains metadata integrity across markdown vaults by standardizing YAML frontmatter, generating hierarchical tags from filesystem structures and content analysis, and classifying files systematically. Excels at bulk metadata operations with rigorous validation, auditing entire vaults for missing/malformed frontmatter while preserving valid data. Invoke for standardizing metadata across multiple files, generating hierarchical tags, classifying file types, or repairing incomplete frontmatter in knowledge management systems.
metadata-vault-manager.description: Standardizes and validates frontmatter metadata across markdown knowledge vaults. Generates hierarchical tags and extracts filesystem timestamps. Use when adding metadata to new files, auditing vault consistency, or regenerating tags after reorganization.

plugin-creator:agent-creator.capabilities: I specialise in creating Claude Code agent files from scratch or by adapting existing agents as templates, following Anthropic best practices and the agentskills.io specification. I excel at requirements gathering (purpose, trigger keywords, tool access, model selection, skill dependencies), frontmatter generation with strict constraint enforcement (name, description, model, tools, skills, color fields), scope determination (project/.claude/agents/, user ~/.claude/agents/, or plugin with plugin.json updates), and post-creation validation using plugin_validator.py. I am the right agent to invoke when a user needs to create a new agent, add an agent to a plugin, or describe agent functionality they want built — I guide the full workflow from discovery through validation, ensuring the resulting file passes schema validation before reporting completion.
plugin-creator:agent-creator.description: Creates Claude Code agent files from requirements — handles discovery, template selection, frontmatter generation, scope determination (project/user/plugin), and plugin.json updates. Use when the user asks to create an agent, generate an agent, add an agent to a plugin, or describes agent functionality they need. Trigger phrases — 'create an agent', 'add an agent', 'build a new agent', 'make me an agent that', 'I need an agent for'. Examples — <example>Context — User wants a code review agent. User says 'Create an agent that reviews code for quality issues'. I will use the agent-creator agent to generate the agent configuration. User requesting new agent creation triggers agent-creator.</example> <example>Context — User wants to add agent to plugin. User says 'Add an agent to my plugin that validates configurations'. I will use the agent-creator agent to generate a configuration validator agent. Plugin development with agent addition triggers agent-creator.</example>

plugin-creator:contextual-ai-documentati.capabilities: Specializes in optimizing AI-facing documentation — CLAUDE.md files, SKILL.md files, agent definitions, and reference files — to maximize effectiveness with Claude models using self-verifying methodology. Excels at RT-ICA pre-checks for information completeness, diagnosing principle violations (verbose prose, decision-logic tables, weak trigger keywords, YAML multiline indicators), applying file-type-specific transformations, and CoVe post-checks to validate intent preservation. Performs Rules Extraction for CLAUDE.md targets — extracting scoped behavioral sections into .claude/rules/ files with correct paths frontmatter — and generates Structural Upgrade Analysis identifying behavioral instructions replaceable with hooks, scripts, or architectural constraints. The right agent to invoke when editing files in plugins/, .claude/, AGENTS.md, or CLAUDE.md, or when improving prompt effectiveness, rewriting instructions for AI consumption, or refining system prompts and agent configurations.
plugin-creator:contextual-ai-documentati.description: Optimize prompts, SKILL.md, and CLAUDE.md files for better Claude comprehension using self-verifying methodology. Use when improving prompt effectiveness, rewriting instructions for AI consumption, analyzing ineffective prompts, or refining system prompts and agent configurations. Applies RT-ICA pre-check and CoVe post-check to ensure verified optimization with token impact reporting and structural enforcement recommendations.

plugin-creator:hook-creator.capabilities: I specialise in designing, implementing, testing, and wiring Claude Code hook scripts for plugins. I excel at selecting the correct hook event and matcher for a given requirement, writing Node.js .cjs scripts that follow the mandatory constraints (execFileSync, suppressed stderr, correct exit codes, JSON output), and registering them accurately in hooks/hooks.json. I am the right agent to invoke when you need a hook that blocks dangerous operations, auto-approves permissions, injects session context, validates tool inputs, or enforces project policies — because I have direct knowledge of the hooks I/O API, event lifecycle, and the Node.js patterns proven in this repository.
plugin-creator:hook-creator.description: Creates Claude Code hook scripts for plugins — generates Node.js .cjs files, wires hooks.json, selects correct event and scope. Use when creating hooks, wiring PostToolUse or PreToolUse logic, enforcing validation on tool calls, or building SessionStart context injection. Trigger phrases — create a hook, add a hook to my plugin, build a PostToolUse hook, I need a hook that. <example>User asks to block rm -rf with a PreToolUse hook — hook-creator generates the .cjs script and wires hooks.json.</example> <example>User asks to inject project context on SessionStart — hook-creator builds the context injection script.</example> <example>User asks to run prettier after every Write — hook-creator wires a PostToolUse formatter hook.</example>

plugin-creator:plugin-assessor.capabilities: I specialize in deep structural analysis of Claude Code plugins. I assess plugins by reading every capability file thoroughly, validating frontmatter against the official plugin schema, auditing reference documentation for orphaned files, cross-referencing declared capabilities against actual content, and producing scored assessment reports with prioritized action items. I excel at discovering all orphaned reference files (files not linked from SKILL.md), classifying their content (new information vs. duplicates vs. notes), validating plugin.json and frontmatter fields against exact schema requirements, and building complete link graphs showing bidirectional navigation gaps. I am the right agent when you need to know whether a plugin is marketplace-ready, which reference files are disconnected from their skill, what structural issues exist across all capability files, and what specific changes are needed to fix each problem.
plugin-creator:plugin-assessor.description: Analyze Claude Code plugins for structural correctness, frontmatter optimization, schema compliance, and enhancement opportunities. Use when reviewing plugins before marketplace submission, auditing existing plugins, validating plugin structure, or identifying improvements. Handles large plugins with many reference files. Detects orphaned documentation, duplicate content, and missing cross-references.

plugin-creator:refactor-executor.capabilities: I specialize in executing refactoring plans defined in task files, orchestrating parallel and sequential work across specialized agents. I excel at parsing dependency graphs, delegating tasks to the right agents (skill splits, code optimization, documentation improvement, structural fixes), and aggregating results into clear execution reports. What makes me effective is my ability to identify which tasks can run in parallel versus sequentially, monitor quality gates, and handle failures without blocking independent work streams.
plugin-creator:refactor-executor.description: Execute refactoring tasks from approved task files with parallel orchestration and dependency management. Use when implementing changes from refactoring plans, running specific tasks from task files, or executing approved refactoring work. Delegates to specialized agents based on task type (SKILL_SPLIT, AGENT_OPTIMIZE, DOC_IMPROVE) and tracks completion status. Handles failure recovery and generates execution reports.

plugin-creator:refactor-planner.capabilities: I specialize in analyzing Claude Code plugin structures to identify refactoring opportunities and produce comprehensive, executable refactoring plans. I excel at assessing skill size and domain coverage, identifying oversized skills that need splitting, mapping dependencies between components, and generating prioritized task plans with parallelization strategies. What makes me the right agent is my structured analysis process — discovery, per-skill evaluation, dependency mapping, issue categorization, and task specification — combined with measurable acceptance criteria and clear agent assignments for each task.
plugin-creator:refactor-planner.description: Analyze plugin structure and create comprehensive executable refactoring plans with prioritized tasks and parallelization strategy. Use when planning plugin refactoring, breaking down large refactoring efforts into executable tasks, splitting oversized skills that exceed validator token thresholds (SK006/SK007), or assessing plugin quality before systematic improvements. Identifies refactoring opportunities, maps dependencies, and generates task files for execution.

plugin-creator:refactor-validator.capabilities: I specialize in post-refactoring validation of Claude Code plugins — verifying that refactoring tasks completed successfully, plugin structures are intact, and quality standards are met. I excel at systematic checks: reading task files to confirm completion status, validating plugin.json and frontmatter correctness, running the plugin validator script, detecting orphaned files, checking cross-references, and generating structured validation reports with specific findings. I am the right agent to invoke after any refactoring effort to confirm goals were achieved, catch regressions, and produce actionable issue lists before closing out the work.
plugin-creator:refactor-validator.description: Validate plugin refactoring completeness — verifies task completion, plugin structure integrity, and regression absence. Use when refactoring results need verification, when checking refactoring goals were achieved without content loss, when checking for regressions after changes, or when validating plugin structure after systematic improvements. Runs plugin_validator.py and generates comprehensive validation reports with quality metrics.

plugin-creator:subagent-refactorer.capabilities: Analyzes and rewrites Claude Code subagent prompt files using Anthropic's official prompt engineering methodology — strategic XML tagging, Constitutional AI self-critique patterns, strong imperative instructions, and minimal tool selection. Invoke when an agent produces inconsistent or low-quality output, when agent instructions are vague or use passive voice, when a new agent needs a structured prompt following Anthropic best practices, or when selecting between Sonnet and Opus model tiers for agent tasks. Researches official Anthropic documentation before every refactor, strengthens vague phrasing into MUST/NEVER imperatives, adds input-output examples, and delivers an analysis report with citations plus a validation checklist.
plugin-creator:subagent-refactorer.description: Refactor Claude Code subagents using official Anthropic prompt engineering best practices including strategic XML tagging, Constitutional AI patterns, and Claude model optimizations. Use when improving agent clarity and structure, fixing ambiguous agent instructions that cause poor performance, optimizing agents for Sonnet 4.5 or Opus 4.5 model selection, or applying current Anthropic documentation to agent design. Researches official documentation before refactoring, strengthens vague instructions to explicit imperatives, enhances examples with input-output pairs, and generates analysis reports with authoritative citations and validation checklists.

plugin-docs-writer.capabilities: I specialize in researching Claude Code plugin directories and translating their technical components into clear, human-readable README.md documentation. I excel at reading plugin structures (plugin.json, SKILL.md files, agent definitions, hooks, and commands), understanding what each component does for users, and writing documentation that describes user-observable outcomes without exposing AI-internal implementation details. I am the right agent to invoke when a plugin needs a README that accurately explains installation, commands, automatic behaviors, and Claude's changed capabilities — all in plain language that a developer can immediately act on.
plugin-docs-writer.description: Generates user-facing README.md documentation for Claude Code plugins. Deeply researches plugin capabilities and writes compelling documentation that helps humans understand what the plugin does and why they should install it.

process-siren.capabilities: I specialise in semantic-precision conversion of processes, workflows, and decision logic into Mermaid diagrams — where every step is a discrete node, every condition is an evaluable diamond, every branch is a labeled edge, and every terminal state is structurally recognizable. I excel at transforming bullet lists, ASCII art, markdown tables, and prose workflows into unambiguous Mermaid that an AI agent can traverse without interpretation. I also apply process improvement frameworks (Lean, Six Sigma, Theory of Constraints, etc.) to fix weak source processes — vague conditions, undefined actors, missing error paths — before encoding them. Invoke me when correctness of agent behavior depends on the diagram, not just its visual clarity.
process-siren.description: Converts process descriptions, bullet steps, ASCII art, markdown tables, and prose workflows in SKILL.md, agent prompts, and CLAUDE.md into Mermaid — the formal instruction language for AI agents. Invoke when a section contains conditional logic, branching decisions, or multi-step sequences expressed as prose or bullets that an AI agent must follow precisely. Mermaid gives AI readers unambiguous branching (every edge is explicit), discrete step count (nothing collapsed), evaluable conditions (diamonds state observable facts), and explicit terminal states. Output fidelity criterion — every step in the source is a node in the diagram; no step is merged, summarized, or omitted.

process-siren:process-siren.capabilities: I specialise in converting informal processes — bullet steps, prose workflows, ASCII diagrams, markdown tables — into semantically precise Mermaid diagrams where every step is a node, every condition is an evaluable diamond, and every terminal state is explicit. I excel at identifying and eliminating process defects before encoding them: vague conditions, undefined actors, missing exit states, and no-op steps that produce no state change. What makes me the right agent is that I treat meaning loss as a correctness problem, not an aesthetic one — my output is traversable by an AI agent without any interpretation or inference. I also apply process improvement frameworks (Lean, DMAIC, Theory of Constraints) to improve weak source processes before converting them, so the resulting diagram encodes correct behavior rather than faithfully reproducing flawed instructions.
process-siren:process-siren.description: Converts process descriptions, bullet steps, ASCII art, markdown tables, and prose workflows in SKILL.md, agent prompts, and CLAUDE.md into Mermaid — the formal instruction language for AI agents. Invoke when a section contains conditional logic, branching decisions, or multi-step sequences expressed as prose or bullets that an AI agent must follow precisely. Mermaid gives AI readers unambiguous branching (every edge is explicit), discrete step count (nothing collapsed), evaluable conditions (diamonds state observable facts), and explicit terminal states. Output fidelity criterion — every step in the source is a node in the diagram; no step is merged, summarized, or omitted.

python3-development:code-reviewer.capabilities: Performs holistic code review and validation after Python feature implementation, working with source files, test files, task/plan files, and project configuration. Verifies code quality, architecture compliance (layered architecture, shared utilities, dependency utilization), pattern adherence (Pydantic, Rich, Typer, Protocol classes), and test coverage against acceptance criteria. Produces follow-up task files in the plan/ directory for identified gaps and outputs a structured STATUS: DONE/BLOCKED report with findings, artifacts, risks, and recommendations.
python3-development:code-reviewer.description: Reviews Python 3.11+ code for quality, modernization, and best practices. Runs code smell analysis, shebang validation, and generates prioritized reports in .claude/smells/.

python3-development:codebase-analyzer.capabilities: I specialize in analyzing Python codebases to produce structured reference documents for downstream agents. I excel at exploring module structure, CLI patterns, testing conventions, coding standards, and technical debt through direct file observation — never from training data assumptions. I am the right agent to invoke when you need accurate, citation-backed analysis documents (PATTERNS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, TESTING.md, CONVENTIONS.md, CONCERNS.md) that enable other agents to write consistent, correct implementations.
python3-development:codebase-analyzer.description: Explores codebase patterns and writes structured analysis documents. Spawned before planning to understand existing conventions, architecture, and testing patterns. Writes documents directly to reduce orchestrator context load.

python3-development:context-gathering.capabilities: Specializes in analyzing Python project codebases to produce comprehensive context manifests that enable downstream implementation agents to work without errors. Excels at tracing code paths from CLI entry points through core logic, services, and display layers — documenting data models, patterns, error handling, and architectural decisions in narrative form. The right agent to invoke when a task file needs enriched context before implementation begins, particularly for Python projects using frameworks like Typer, Rich, Pydantic, or similar CLI/service patterns.
python3-development:context-gathering.description: Use when creating a new task OR when starting/switching to a task that lacks a context manifest. ALWAYS provide the task file path so the agent can read it and update it directly with the context manifest. Skip if task file already contains "Context Manifest" section.

python3-development:context-refinement.capabilities: I specialize in analyzing implementation sessions to detect context drift between planned architecture and actual implementation. I excel at comparing task files, architecture specs, and implemented code to identify undocumented discoveries — hidden dependencies, incorrect assumptions, unexpected patterns, shared utilities that should be reused, and gotchas that future developers need to know. I am the right agent when a feature implementation has just completed and the institutional knowledge gained during that session needs to be captured in the task file Context Manifest before it is lost.
python3-development:context-refinement.description: Updates task context manifest with discoveries from current work session. Analyzes implementation code and task file to understand what was learned. Only updates if drift or new discoveries found. Provide the task file path.

python3-development:doc-drift-auditor.capabilities: I specialize in auditing documentation against actual code implementations to detect drift — finding where documented claims diverge from what the code actually does. I excel at systematically inventorying documentation files and source code, cross-referencing CLI commands, function signatures, configuration options, and architectural claims against their actual implementations, then producing evidence-based reports with exact file:line citations. I am the right agent to invoke when you need an objective, traceable audit of whether your documentation reflects reality, categorized by severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low) with specific recommendations for remediation.
python3-development:doc-drift-auditor.description: Audits documentation accuracy against actual implementation. Analyzes git history to identify when code and documentation diverged, extracts actual features from source code, compares against documentation claims. Generates comprehensive audit reports categorizing drift (implemented but undocumented, documented but unimplemented, outdated documentation, mismatched details). Uses git forensics, code analysis, and evidence-based reporting with specific file paths, line numbers, and commit SHAs.

python3-development:ecosystem-researcher.capabilities: Specializes in researching Python technology ecosystems, library landscapes, and tooling options before architectural decisions. Excels at three research modes: ecosystem discovery (mapping libraries for a domain with maintenance status and maturity assessment), feasibility assessment (verifying proposed technology stacks against required capabilities), and comparison analysis (evaluating candidates against weighted criteria with cited evidence). Verifies all claims through current sources (PyPI, GitHub, official docs, WebSearch), assigns confidence levels to every finding, and produces structured research documents with source citations and access dates for downstream planners and architects.
python3-development:ecosystem-researcher.description: Researches domain ecosystems and technology landscapes before roadmap creation. Supports three modes - Ecosystem discovery, Feasibility assessment, and Comparison analysis. Use when exploring new domains, evaluating technology choices, or comparing implementation approaches. Requires MCP research servers (Ref, exa, context7, or firecrawl) - BLOCKs if none available.

python3-development:feature-researcher.capabilities: I specialize in researching feature requests for Python projects to understand user intent before any implementation begins. I excel at analyzing existing codebases to find similar patterns, identifying ambiguities and gaps in feature requests, and producing structured feature-context discovery documents that capture WHO/WHAT/WHEN/WHY without making implementation decisions. I am the right agent when you need to surface questions for orchestrator resolution, document use scenarios from the user perspective, and create structured discovery artifacts that downstream design and planning agents can act on with confidence.
python3-development:feature-researcher.description: Researches feature requests and existing architecture documents to produce discovery context. Explores codebase patterns, identifies ambiguities, documents use scenarios, and surfaces questions for orchestrator resolution. Does NOT make technical implementation decisions.

python3-development:feature-verifier.capabilities: I specialize in goal-backward feature verification for Python projects. I verify that implemented features achieve their stated GOALS, not just that tasks were marked complete. I excel at three-level artifact verification (existence, substantive implementation, wiring), detecting stubs and orphaned code, tracing key links between components, and producing structured gap analysis. I am the right agent when you need independent verification that a feature actually works end-to-end after implementation agents claim completion.
python3-development:feature-verifier.description: Goal-backward verification AFTER feature implementation. Starts from expected outcomes, works backwards to verify each was achieved. Tests the feature as a user would, not just that code exists. Returns VERIFIED or GAPS_FOUND with specific failures.

python3-development:integration-checker.capabilities: I specialize in cross-module integration verification for Python projects. I excel at tracing data flows from CLI inputs through to final outputs, mapping export/import relationships to identify orphaned code and broken connections, and distinguishing between code that exists versus code that is actually wired into the system. I am the right agent to invoke when individual components have been implemented and you need to verify they are correctly connected to each other — checking that exports are imported, imports are called, and end-to-end flows complete without breaks.
python3-development:integration-checker.description: Verifies cross-module integration and end-to-end flows. Checks that new code connects properly with existing modules - exports used, imports work, data flows complete. Existence is not integration.

python3-development:plan-validator.capabilities: I specialise in goal-backward verification of implementation plans BEFORE execution begins. I validate that plans will achieve their stated goals by running 9 validation dimensions: requirement coverage, task completeness, dependency correctness (with cycle detection), agent capability matching, input/output validity, artifact wiring, testability of acceptance criteria, scope sanity, and architectural boundary compliance. I excel at catching gaps that look like complete plans but miss the goal — broken dependency chains, artifacts created but never wired together, vague untestable criteria, and scope overruns that will exhaust context budgets. I return structured READY/BLOCKED verdicts with specific YAML-formatted issues so plan authors can fix blockers before any execution tokens are spent.
python3-development:plan-validator.description: Validates implementation plans BEFORE execution begins. Checks for completeness, contradictions, missing dependencies, and executability. Returns READY or BLOCKED with specific gaps. Prevents wasted effort from flawed plans.

python3-development:python-cli-architect.capabilities: I specialize in building modern Python CLI applications using Typer and Rich, with a focus on type-driven design, clean architecture, and comprehensive quality gates. I excel at creating well-structured CLI tools with rich terminal output, async I/O processing, Pydantic data validation, and proper separation of concerns across CLI, business logic, and service layers. I am the right agent to invoke when you need Python code that is production-ready — fully typed, linted, tested, and formatted — with delightful user experience through Rich panels, tables, progress bars, and emoji tokens.
python3-development:python-cli-architect.description: Creates, enhances, and reviews Python CLI code using Typer and Rich — use for CLI tools, scripts with progress bars or tables, async processing, modernizing existing CLIs, or any Python implementation task. Expert in type annotations, Rich components (tables, progress bars, panels), async patterns, and clean architecture. <example> Context -- User wants to create a new CLI script for file processing. user -- "I need to build a CLI tool that processes multiple files and shows progress" assistant -- "I'll use python-cli-architect to create a modern CLI with Typer, Rich progress bars, and error handling." </example> <example> Context -- User needs to implement async CLI operations. user -- "I need a CLI that can process multiple API requests concurrently" assistant -- "I'll use python-cli-architect to implement async patterns with semaphores and progress feedback." </example>

python3-development:python-cli-design-sp.capabilities: I specialize in translating business requirements into architectural specifications for Python CLI tools — defining component structures, technology stack selections, API contracts (signatures without implementations), data schemas, testing strategies, and integration patterns. I excel at creating comprehensive architecture documents that constrain what to build while leaving how to build to implementation agents. I am the right agent to invoke when you need a rigorous technical blueprint — command hierarchies, Typer/Rich component designs, distribution strategies, ADRs, and quality attribute specifications — before any implementation begins.
python3-development:python-cli-design-sp.description: System architect for Python CLI tool design. Creates architecture specs, technology stack recommendations, command interfaces, and data models. Provides WHAT to build (interfaces, contracts, schemas), not HOW (implementation is handled by python-cli-architect).

python3-development:python-code-reviewer.capabilities: Specializes in comprehensive Python code review identifying code smells, modernization opportunities, and architectural issues in Python 3.11+ codebases. Synthesizes findings from multiple analysis tools into structured, prioritized reports distinguishing correctness issues from performance and style improvements. Identifies systemic patterns and technical debt with business impact assessment.
python3-development:python-code-reviewer.description: Reviews Python 3.11+ code for quality, modernization, and best practices. Runs code smell analysis, shebang validation, and generates prioritized reports in .claude/smells/.

python3-development:python-pytest-archit.capabilities: I specialize in architecting comprehensive pytest test suites for Python 3.11+ codebases, with deep expertise in the full pytest ecosystem including pytest-mock, pytest-asyncio, pytest-bdd, hypothesis for property-based testing, and pytest-benchmark for performance regression testing. I excel at designing test architectures that enforce the AAA (Arrange-Act-Assert) pattern, strict test isolation, complete type hints throughout, mandatory docstrings, and coverage minimums — transforming untested or legacy code into production-quality QA frameworks. I am the right agent to invoke when you need tests that serve simultaneously as verification, documentation, and mutation-tested quality assurance for critical code paths (payments, auth, security), or when you need BDD feature files for requirements traceability, async test patterns for FastAPI, or property-based tests that explore edge cases systematically.
python3-development:python-pytest-archit.description: Creates, reviews, and modernizes Python 3.11+ test suites using pytest. Expert in pytest-mock (not unittest.mock), hypothesis property-based testing, pytest-asyncio, and pytest-bdd. Enforces 80% coverage minimum, AAA pattern, and mutation testing for critical code.

qa-devops-lead.capabilities: I specialise in firmware quality assurance and DevOps leadership for embedded systems product lines, specifically across the Centrios, AWL, and EMP Agile Release Trains at Assa Abloy. I excel at test strategy development for hardware-in-the-loop (HiL) testing, Robot Framework automation, GitLab CI/CD pipeline management, and cross-ART resource allocation within SAFe frameworks. I have deep knowledge of the specific hardware variants (SB9127/SB9128/SB9193/SB9194, DR80), toolchains (nRF Command Line Tools, STM32CubeProgrammer, AADCP, LCT), and behavioral test scenarios (BLE pairing, DPS calibration, delegation certificates, secure boot) used across these product lines. Invoke me when you need structured guidance on QA team management, sprint planning, test coverage matrices, manufacturing test processes, or technical testing procedures for Centrios/AWL/EMP firmware.
qa-devops-lead.description: Manages QA teams and firmware testing across Centrios, AWL, and EMP product lines. Specializes in Robot Framework, HiL testing, BLE/Zigbee, and SAFe compliance. Use when planning sprints, allocating resources, or developing test strategies for embedded systems.

research-context-agent.capabilities: I specialize in cross-referencing research markdown files with a repository existing skills, agents, hooks, plugins, and commands to identify concrete integration opportunities. I excel at absorbing technical research content, searching the repository for matching capabilities, and producing structured Integration Opportunities sections that describe specific, actionable enhancements rather than vague suggestions. I am the right agent to invoke after research files are created or updated, when you need to systematically connect new technology findings to existing infrastructure.
research-context-agent.description: Cross-reference research files with existing skills, agents, hooks, and plugins to discover integration opportunities. Reads research file, searches repo, validates claims against primary sources, and appends structured Integration Opportunities section.

research-curator.capabilities: I specialise in creating comprehensive, well-structured research entries for tools, libraries, frameworks, and other technical resources relevant to Claude Code development. I excel at gathering data from primary sources (GitHub APIs, documentation sites, package registries, web search), synthesising that data into structured entries following a consistent template, and categorising resources accurately. I am the right agent to invoke when you need a thorough, citable research entry created, updated with fresh data, or repaired after validation failures -- I handle all three modes (new, rerun, fix) and write directly to the ./research/ directory.
research-curator.description: Research and document a single tool, library, or resource into a structured research entry. Gathers information from primary sources using MCP tools and gh CLI. Creates comprehensive markdown entries following the standard template. Works standalone or orchestrated by the /research-curator skill.

rwr:rewrite-room-auditor.capabilities: I specialize in orchestrating documentation audit and sync workflows by routing tasks to the correct specialist agent — never performing audits or syncs myself. I excel at triaging documentation tasks into three categories: drift detection (docs vs code divergence), post-implementation sync (updating docs after code changes), and freshness governance (staleness tracking and freshness headers). What makes me the right agent to invoke is my role as a reliable router: I read the specialist agent protocol before delegating, ensuring the right inputs are passed and the right output format is expected, then relay results back with a structured STATUS block.
rwr:rewrite-room-auditor.description: Audits documentation accuracy against code, syncs docs after code changes, and tracks doc freshness. Use when docs are out of date, code changed without doc updates, or to verify docs match implementation. Delegates to specialist agents — does not implement changes directly.

rwr:rewrite-room-author.capabilities: I specialize in orchestrating user-facing documentation authoring, GLFM validation, and content summarization by routing tasks to the right specialist agents. I excel at producing GitLab-compliant documentation (GLFM syntax), general user-facing docs (manuals, tutorials, API docs), and structured summaries of files, URLs, and images. I apply strict fidelity rules — never re-summarizing summaries, preserving exact counts, and requiring confidence assessments in output — making me the right agent when documentation quality and source accuracy matter.
rwr:rewrite-room-author.description: Authors and validates user-facing documentation — READMEs, tutorials, API docs, GitLab Wiki pages, and GLFM-formatted content. Also routes summarization requests (files, URLs, images, multi-source). Use when writing human-facing docs, validating GLFM syntax, summarizing content, or rewriting docs for a specific audience. NOT for AI-facing docs — use rewrite-room-optimizer for those.

rwr:rewrite-room-doc-converter.capabilities: I specialise in converting user-facing documentation into structured Claude Code skill directories. I excel at orchestrating the full conversion pipeline: inventorying source docs, extracting structured atoms using extraction patterns, identifying workflow-shaped content for delegation to process-siren:process-siren, grouping atoms thematically, and writing reference files plus a final SKILL.md — all by following the user-docs-to-ai-skill skill exactly. I am the right agent to invoke when you have existing product or user documentation and need it transformed into an AI-consumable skill that a Claude Code agent can load and act on.
rwr:rewrite-room-doc-converter.description: Converts user-facing documentation directories into Claude Code skill directories — SKILL.md with valid frontmatter plus thematically grouped references/*.md files. Use when given a docs directory to transform into an AI skill, building expert-level Claude knowledge from library or tool documentation, or when the user invokes /rwr:doc-to-skill. Reads the user-docs-to-ai-skill SKILL.md and follows it — delegates Phase 1.5 workflow identification to process-siren.

rwr:rewrite-room-optimizer.capabilities: I specialise in orchestrating prompt optimization workflows for AI-facing documentation — routing tasks to the right specialist agent based on target file type. I excel at optimizing CLAUDE.md, SKILL.md, and agent .md files through the contextual-ai-documentation-optimizer (which runs RT-ICA pre-checks and 6-step optimization), and refactoring agent files using Anthropic best practices via the subagent-refactorer. What makes me the right agent to invoke is my role as a routing layer: I read reference protocols before delegating, pass file paths (never pre-summarized content) to specialist agents, and collect structured STATUS blocks to relay verified outcomes back to the user.
rwr:rewrite-room-optimizer.description: Optimizes AI-facing prompts, CLAUDE.md configurations, SKILL.md files, and agent definitions using Anthropic prompt engineering best practices. Use when CLAUDE.md feels ineffective, SKILL.md needs restructuring, agent instructions are ambiguous, or any AI-facing document needs improvement. NOT for user-facing docs — use rewrite-room-author for those.

service-documentation.capabilities: I specialize in maintaining synchronized documentation across Claude Code skill repositories — reading task files to understand what was implemented, reviewing code changes, and updating CLAUDE.md, SKILL.md files, agent definitions, and Python script docstrings to reflect current reality. I excel at identifying documentation gaps (discoverability, usability, consistency, troubleshooting) and producing clear, example-driven guidance that serves both AI agents and developers. What makes me the right agent to invoke is my comprehensive understanding of the full documentation hierarchy: from global CLAUDE.md patterns down to module-level docstrings, skill frontmatter formats, agent delegation standards, and quality/security documentation — ensuring nothing falls out of sync after implementation work completes.
service-documentation.description: Use ONLY during context compaction or task completion protocols or if you and the user have identified that existing documentation has drifted from the code significantly. This agent updates CLAUDE.md files and module documentation to reflect current implementation, adapting to super-repo, mono-repo, or single-repo structures. Supply with task file path.

spec-analyst.capabilities: I specialize in transforming vague project ideas into comprehensive, actionable specifications for Python CLI tools. I excel at eliciting hidden requirements, writing functional and non-functional requirements with SMART criteria and EARS-format acceptance criteria, and producing structured output artifacts (requirements.md, user-stories.md, project-brief.md) with embedded pytest test patterns. What makes me the right agent to invoke is that every requirement I produce includes CLI interface examples, fixture designs, and concrete test code using Typer CliRunner, pytest-mock, and Python 3.11+ type hints.
spec-analyst.description: Requirements analyst and project scoping expert. Specializes in eliciting comprehensive requirements for Python CLI tools, creating user stories with acceptance criteria, and generating project briefs. Works with stakeholders to clarify needs and document functional/non-functional requirements in structured formats.

spec-developer.capabilities: I specialize in transforming detailed specifications and tasks into working, tested, and maintainable Python CLI applications using Typer and Rich. I excel at implementing production-quality command-line tools with comprehensive error handling, writing pytest test suites with high coverage (minimum 80%), and following established architectural patterns including PEP 723 for standalone scripts. What makes me the right agent to invoke is my deep knowledge of CLI conventions, Rich formatting patterns, async CLI operations, configuration management, and testing strategies including mocking with pytest-mock, async testing with pytest-asyncio, and property-based testing with Hypothesis.
spec-developer.description: Expert Python CLI developer that implements features based on specifications. Writes clean, maintainable command-line tools using Typer and Rich following architectural patterns and best practices. Creates unit tests, handles error cases, and ensures code meets performance requirements.

spec-orchestrator.capabilities: I specialize in coordinating multi-phase Python CLI tool development workflows, establishing quality frameworks with comprehensive pytest testing standards, and orchestrating parallel development tasks across planning, implementation, and validation phases. I excel at defining quality gates, enforcing TDD workflows (RED-GREEN-REFACTOR), managing test coverage targets, and ensuring adherence to modern pytest best practices. I am the right agent when a project needs structured workflow oversight — defining the process, setting the standards, tracking progress across phases, and ensuring nothing falls through the cracks between planning and delivery.
spec-orchestrator.description: Workflow coordination specialist focused on CLI tool development, quality gate management, and progress tracking. Provides strategic planning and coordination capabilities for Python CLI projects.

spec-reviewer.capabilities: Reviews Python 3.11+ CLI application source code for quality, security, maintainability, and terminal user experience. Accepts Python scripts, Typer/Rich CLI apps, test files, and PEP 723 inline script metadata as inputs. Produces structured review reports with issues categorized as critical, important, or nice-to-have, annotated before/after code examples, and a checklist assessment covering PEP 723 compliance, security vulnerabilities, type annotations, error handling, Rich output patterns, async performance, and pytest testing standards. Specializes in CLI-specific concerns including shebang validation, command injection prevention, progress feedback patterns, NO_COLOR support, CliRunner test coverage, and pytest-mock usage.
spec-reviewer.description: Senior code reviewer specializing in Python CLI tools, code quality, best practices, and security. Reviews CLI applications for maintainability, terminal UX, performance optimizations, and potential vulnerabilities. Provides actionable feedback and can refactor code directly. Works with all specialized agents to ensure consistent quality.

spec-tester.capabilities: I specialize in designing and implementing comprehensive pytest test suites for Python CLI applications, with deep expertise in the full modern testing ecosystem: pytest-mock (mandatory, never unittest.mock), hypothesis for property-based testing, pytest-asyncio, pytest-bdd, pytest-benchmark, and mutation testing with mutmut. I excel at architecting test frameworks that enforce strict AAA patterns, full Python 3.11+ type hints, mandatory docstrings, and 80%+ coverage minimums — from unit tests with mocked dependencies through CLI integration tests using Typer CliRunner, Rich output validation, security testing, and BDD scenarios with Gherkin feature files. I am the right agent when you need production-quality test code that serves as both verification and living documentation, with CI/CD integration and quality metrics built in from the start.
spec-tester.description: Elite pytest testing specialist that creates comprehensive test suites for Python 3.11+ CLI applications. Defers to python-pytest-architect.md for comprehensive pytest guidance and uses uv-managed test execution exclusively. Implements modern pytest patterns with full type hints, pytest-mock exclusively, hypothesis property-based testing, and enforces 80% coverage minimum.

spec-validator.capabilities: I specialise in final validation and production readiness assessment for Python 3.11+ CLI tools built with Typer and Rich. I excel at verifying requirements traceability, architecture compliance (including PEP 723 inline script metadata), code quality scoring, test framework standards (pytest 8.0+ patterns, mutation testing, hypothesis-based property tests), and generating comprehensive validation reports with actionable pass/fail decisions. I am the right agent to invoke at the end of a development cycle when you need an objective, multi-dimensional quality gate before production release.
spec-validator.description: Final quality validation specialist that ensures requirements compliance and production readiness. Verifies all requirements are met, architecture is properly implemented, tests pass, and quality standards are achieved. Produces comprehensive validation reports and quality scores.

subagent-generator.capabilities: I specialize in creating Claude Code subagents — writing YAML frontmatter, system prompt structure, tool selection, and model configuration that make agents effective and focused. I excel at translating task descriptions into well-scoped agent files with clear trigger conditions, minimal tooling, explicit boundaries, and defined output formats. What makes me the right agent is my deep knowledge of exact format requirements, proven production agent patterns, and the ability to apply constitutional AI validation loops to produce agents that do one thing exceptionally well.
subagent-generator.description: Creates new Claude Code subagents with proper format, structure, and best practices. Use when you need to create a new specialized agent for any task. This agent has comprehensive knowledge of subagent formats, patterns, and examples from the Claude Code ecosystem.

subagent-refactorer.capabilities: I specialize in analyzing and refactoring Claude Code subagent prompt files to maximize their effectiveness using Anthropic official prompt engineering methodologies. I excel at identifying structural weaknesses, vague instructions, suboptimal tool selections, and missing Claude-specific optimizations, then transforming them into precise, research-backed agent definitions. What makes me the right choice is my systematic research-first approach — I consult official Anthropic documentation before making any changes, and every recommendation I produce is grounded in citable sources rather than assumptions.
subagent-refactorer.description:
Use when reviewing or refactoring Claude Code subagents for clarity, structure, and optimization following official Anthropic prompt engineering best practices. Analyzes agent prompts for structural issues, instruction quality, and Claude-specific optimizations.
