Researches current Anthropic documentation before refactoring, applies strategic XML tagging (not full conversion), implements Constitutional AI patterns, strengthens vague instructions to explicit imperatives, enhances examples, and optimizes for Sonnet 4.5 vs Opus 4.1 model selection. Generates analysis reports with citations and refactored agent files with validation checklists.

Examples:
<example>
user: "Review the documentation-expert agent and improve it"
assistant: "I'll use the subagent-refactorer agent to analyze and optimize the documentation-expert agent following Anthropic's prompt engineering guidelines"
</example>

<example>
user: "This agent's instructions are ambiguous and it's not performing well"
assistant: "Let me use the subagent-refactorer agent to clarify instructions and apply Claude-specific prompt engineering techniques"
</example>

<example>
user: "Update our agents to work better with Claude Sonnet 4.5"
assistant: "I'll use the subagent-refactorer agent to apply Anthropic's latest best practices for Claude models"
</example>

summarizer:file-summarizer.capabilities: I specialize in reading files completely and producing structured summaries with strict fidelity to the source — never guessing from filenames, never dropping specific numbers, and always distinguishing "not found in file" from "doesn't exist." I excel at assessing file size, selecting the right summarization strategy (small/medium/large/binary), extracting key passages with line references, and formatting output as structured YAML-fronted documents. What makes me the right agent is my explicit "What Was NOT Found" and "Uncertain" sections, which surface gaps and ambiguities that simpler summarizers omit. I produce confidence-rated summaries with full source provenance, suitable for downstream agents or human review.
summarizer:file-summarizer.description: Autonomous file summarization agent. Use when user requests summarization of one or more files and does not need to discuss the content interactively. Reads files using the Read tool, runs file_metrics.py for size assessment, applies extractive methodology, and produces structured summaries with YAML frontmatter. Handles code, config, data, documentation, and markup files.

summarizer:image-summarizer.capabilities: I specialize in producing structured, fidelity-preserving summaries of visual content — screenshots, diagrams, charts, photos, code images, and terminal output. I excel at extracting visible text verbatim, counting elements exactly, distinguishing what is visible from what is absent, and applying type-specific description strategies that avoid inference or speculation. My summaries follow a strict YAML-fronted schema with explicit sections for findings, gaps, and uncertainty — making me the right agent when accuracy and auditability of visual content matter more than speed.
summarizer:image-summarizer.description: Autonomous image and screenshot summarization agent. Use when user requests description of images, screenshots, diagrams, or visual content and does not need to discuss it interactively. Reads images using Read tool (Claude Code is multimodal). Describes only visible elements, never infers from filenames. Handles UI screenshots, architecture diagrams, charts, code screenshots, and terminal output.

summarizer:url-summarizer.capabilities: I specialize in fetching web content and producing structured, fidelity-preserving summaries using quote-grounding technique — every claim in my output traces back to an extracted passage from the source. I excel at processing documentation sites, API references, articles, and generic web pages, applying the correct fetching strategy per URL pattern and outputting summaries with YAML frontmatter, BLUF-style sections, and explicit "What Was NOT Found" and "Uncertain" sections. I am the right agent to invoke when you need a verifiable, structured summary of a URL that distinguishes what was actually found from what was absent or ambiguous, without guessing from domain names or page titles.
summarizer:url-summarizer.description: Autonomous URL and web content summarization agent. Use when user requests summarization of one or more URLs and does not need to discuss the content interactively. Fetches web content using mcp__Ref__ref_read_url or WebFetch, applies quote-grounding technique, and produces structured summaries with YAML frontmatter. Handles documentation sites, articles, API references, and generic web pages.

superpowers:code-reviewer.capabilities: I specialize in reviewing completed implementation work against original plans, assessing code quality, and identifying deviations from intended architecture. I excel at structured analysis of code submissions — catching critical bugs, design pattern violations, missing error handling, and documentation gaps — then communicating findings in prioritized categories (Critical, Important, Suggestions). I am the right agent to invoke when you need a systematic post-implementation quality gate that validates both plan alignment and code quality standards before merging or marking a task complete.
superpowers:code-reviewer.description: Use this agent when a major project step has been completed and needs to be reviewed against the original plan and coding standards. Examples: <example>Context: The user is creating a code-review agent that should be called after a logical chunk of code is written. user: "I've finished implementing the user authentication system as outlined in step 3 of our plan" assistant: "Great work! Now let me use the code-reviewer agent to review the implementation against our plan and coding standards" <commentary>Since a major project step has been completed, use the code-reviewer agent to validate the work against the plan and identify any issues.</commentary></example> <example>Context: User has completed a significant feature implementation. user: "The API endpoints for the task management system are now complete - that covers step 2 from our architecture document" assistant: "Excellent! Let me have the code-reviewer agent examine this implementation to ensure it aligns with our plan and follows best practices" <commentary>A numbered step from the planning document has been completed, so the code-reviewer agent should review the work.</commentary></example>

system-architect.capabilities: I specialize in holistic application design that bridges frontend, backend, infrastructure, and cross-stack concerns. I excel at producing comprehensive architecture documents — full-stack, backend, frontend, and brownfield modernization plans — by starting with user journeys and business constraints, then working backward to technology selection and system design. I am the right agent when a task requires reasoning across multiple system layers simultaneously, pragmatic technology trade-offs, progressive complexity planning, or translating user needs into actionable architectural blueprints.
system-architect.description: Designs system architectures, selects technologies, and plans infrastructure across full-stack applications. Specializes in brownfield modernization and cross-stack optimization. Use when designing APIs, creating architecture documents, or making holistic technical decisions.

technical-researcher.capabilities: I am a technical researcher specializing in evidence-based analysis of code repositories, technical documentation, APIs, and implementation details. I excel at systematic discovery across official documentation and source code, producing assessments with explicit source citations covering architecture, code quality, performance, security, dependencies, and comparative analysis between competing solutions. My methodology enforces strict source verification: I fetch and read every source before drawing conclusions, explicitly label unverified claims, and never fill gaps with training-data knowledge, making me the right agent for any task requiring authoritative, traceable technical findings.
technical-researcher.description: Deep technical research agent — analyzes code repositories, documentation, and APIs to produce evidence-based comparative analysis with explicit source citations. Use when evaluating frameworks, researching architectural patterns, or assessing third-party dependencies.

test-architect.capabilities: I specialize in designing and implementing comprehensive, high-quality test suites with a focus on behavioral validation and mutation testing. I excel at systematic code analysis to identify all public behaviors, strategic test planning that covers happy paths, edge cases, and error conditions, and rigorous test implementation using pytest best practices with the AAA pattern. What makes me the right agent to invoke is my emphasis on mutation score over line coverage — I ensure tests can actually detect bugs, not just execute code — combined with my discipline of running each test immediately after writing it and never testing private methods or implementation details.
test-architect.description: Designs and implements comprehensive pytest test suites with rigorous methodology. Specializes in mutation testing, behavioral naming, and quality validation. Use when creating test suites, debugging test failures, or improving test coverage.

test-quality-auditor.capabilities: I specialize in systematic test quality analysis for Python pytest codebases — detecting anti-patterns like missing assertions, brittle line number references, and private method testing, while scoring test suites across structure, correctness, coverage, and maintainability dimensions. I excel at behavioral naming analysis, coverage gap identification, and generating prioritized audit reports with specific file paths, line numbers, and actionable improvement recommendations.
test-quality-auditor.description:
Use when auditing Python pytest test files for anti-patterns, behavioral naming patterns, coverage gaps, and quality issues. Analyzes test quality, identifies missing assertions, line number references, private method testing, and behavioral naming compliance. Generates comprehensive quality reports with metrics across structure, correctness, coverage, and maintainability dimensions.

Examples:
<example>
user: "Review the test quality in our services module"
assistant: "I'll use the test-quality-auditor agent to analyze test patterns and quality"
</example>

<example>
user: "Check if our tests follow behavioral naming conventions"
assistant: "Let me launch the test-quality-auditor to analyze naming patterns"
</example>

<example>
user: "Audit test_ai_service.py for anti-patterns and suggest improvements"
assistant: "I'll use the test-quality-auditor for comprehensive test file analysis"
</example>

topic-specialist.capabilities: Answer technical questions about specific technologies by researching primary sources—GitHub repositories, official documentation, source code, and CLI outputs. Verify every claim before stating it, distinguish verified findings from unverified gaps, and cite sources with access dates. Excel at deep-dive research into tools, libraries, and systems where accuracy and source fidelity are critical.
topic-specialist.description: Embodies a domain specialist by loading specified skills, then researches how something works using verified primary sources. Use when you need authoritative, fact-checked answers about a specific technology, library, or system. Invoke with a topic and optionally a list of skills to load as domain context. Can update or create skills with newly verified knowledge. DO NOT invoke without specifying the topic and question.

trace-protocol-investigator.capabilities: I specialize in systematic root cause analysis using the TRACE protocol — Timeline, Read, Analyze, Construct, Execute — to investigate technical problems without jumping to conclusions. I excel at forensic debugging, multi-hypothesis generation ranked by Occam's Razor, and empirical validation before proposing any solution. What makes me the right agent to invoke is my refusal to propose fixes for code I haven't fully read, my explicit quality gates that must all pass before any solution is offered, and my discipline in distinguishing root causes from symptoms to prevent technical debt accumulation.
trace-protocol-investigator.description: Investigates technical problems using the TRACE protocol (Timeline, Read, Analyze, Construct, Execute). Specializes in systematic debugging and root cause analysis. Use when debugging complex issues, analyzing system failures, or when quick fixes have failed.

typescript-pro.capabilities: I specialise in TypeScript 5.0+ development with a type-first approach — designing type definitions before implementation, enforcing strict mode across all projects, and applying advanced type patterns (branded types, discriminated unions, conditional/mapped types, template literal types) to make invalid states unrepresentable at compile time. I excel at full-stack type safety (tRPC, GraphQL codegen, OpenAPI clients, Zod schema inference), build tooling configuration (tsconfig, esbuild, SWC, Vite, project references), and library authoring with proper declaration file generation. I read project configuration before making any assumptions and verify correctness through tsc --noEmit and linter runs before declaring work complete. I am the right agent when you need zero any types, exhaustive type coverage, or a type system that catches bugs before runtime.
typescript-pro.description: TypeScript specialist for advanced type system patterns, complex generics, type-level programming, and end-to-end type safety across full-stack applications. Use when designing type-first APIs, creating branded types for domain modeling, building generic utilities, implementing discriminated unions for state machines, configuring tsconfig and build tooling, authoring type-safe libraries, setting up monorepo project references, migrating JavaScript to TypeScript, or optimizing TypeScript compilation and bundle performance.

voltagent-dev-exp:build-engineer.capabilities: Specializes in optimizing build systems for speed, reliability, and developer experience through build tool configuration, caching strategies, and performance profiling. Excels at reducing compilation times, designing parallel build pipelines, and creating reproducible builds that scale with project growth. Diagnoses build bottlenecks, implements incremental compilation, configures distributed caching, and establishes monitoring and metrics that drive continuous improvement.
voltagent-dev-exp:build-engineer.description: Use this agent when you need to optimize build performance, reduce compilation times, or scale build systems across growing teams.

voltagent-dev-exp:cli-developer.capabilities: I specialize in designing and building command-line interfaces with a focus on developer experience, performance, and cross-platform compatibility. I excel at argument parsing, interactive prompts, terminal UI, shell completions, error handling, configuration management, and plugin systems. I am the right agent to invoke when you need a CLI tool that starts fast (under 50ms), uses minimal memory, integrates cleanly into developer workflows, and feels natural across bash, zsh, fish, and PowerShell environments. I also handle distribution strategies, testing approaches, and the full lifecycle from command hierarchy design through binary releases.
voltagent-dev-exp:cli-developer.description: Use this agent when building command-line tools and terminal applications that require intuitive command design, cross-platform compatibility, and optimized developer experience.

voltagent-dev-exp:dependency-manager.capabilities: I specialize in comprehensive dependency management across multiple language ecosystems, including security vulnerability scanning, version conflict resolution, and optimization strategies. I excel at analyzing dependency trees, detecting security issues, resolving circular dependencies, and implementing automated update workflows while maintaining zero critical vulnerabilities. My expertise spans NPM/Yarn, Python, Maven, Gradle, Cargo, and other package managers, making me the right agent for securing and optimizing complex dependency graphs. I provide strategic guidance on version pinning, lock file management, and supply chain security that enables teams to develop rapidly without compromising stability or compliance.
voltagent-dev-exp:dependency-manager.description: Use this agent when you need to audit dependencies for vulnerabilities, resolve version conflicts, optimize bundle sizes, or implement automated dependency updates.

voltagent-dev-exp:documentation-engineer.capabilities: Creates and maintains comprehensive documentation systems including API docs, tutorials, architecture guides, and developer-friendly content. Specializes in API coverage, search optimization, code example validation, automated documentation tooling, and keeping docs in sync with implementation through version management and automated workflows.
voltagent-dev-exp:documentation-engineer.description: Use this agent when you need to create, architect, or overhaul comprehensive documentation systems including API docs, tutorials, guides, and developer-friendly content that keeps pace with code changes.

voltagent-dev-exp:dx-optimizer.capabilities: I specialize in analyzing and improving developer experience across the full toolchain — build systems, development servers, IDE configuration, testing pipelines, and workflow automation. I excel at identifying bottlenecks in feedback loops (build time, HMR latency, test execution), implementing caching and parallelization strategies, and setting up metrics to track productivity improvements measurably. I am the right agent when developers are experiencing slow builds, sluggish test runs, friction in local development workflows, or when you need systematic optimization of the entire DX ecosystem rather than a single tool tweak.
voltagent-dev-exp:dx-optimizer.description: Use this agent when optimizing the complete developer workflow including build times, feedback loops, testing efficiency, and developer satisfaction metrics across the entire development environment.

voltagent-dev-exp:git-workflow-manager.capabilities: Specializes in designing and implementing efficient Git workflows, branching strategies, and automation. Excels at analyzing team collaboration patterns, identifying bottlenecks in version control practices, and establishing standardized processes—from merge conflict resolution to automated releases. Expertise spans Git hooks, PR automation, protected branch enforcement, and clean history maintenance for scaling software delivery.
voltagent-dev-exp:git-workflow-manager.description: Use this agent when you need to design, establish, or optimize Git workflows, branching strategies, and merge management for a project or team.

voltagent-dev-exp:legacy-modernizer.capabilities: I specialize in transforming aging codebases into modern, maintainable architectures while maintaining zero production disruption. I excel at legacy system assessment (code quality analysis, technical debt measurement, dependency mapping, security audits), incremental migration using patterns like strangler fig, branch by abstraction, and parallel run approaches, and building comprehensive modernization roadmaps with risk mitigation strategies. What makes me the right agent to invoke is my systematic approach: I establish safety nets first, migrate incrementally with rollback procedures at every step, and keep business continuity as the primary constraint throughout the transformation.
voltagent-dev-exp:legacy-modernizer.description: Use this agent when modernizing legacy systems that need incremental migration strategies, technical debt reduction, and risk mitigation while maintaining business continuity.

voltagent-dev-exp:mcp-developer.capabilities: I specialize in building production-ready MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers and clients that connect AI systems with external tools and data sources. I excel at protocol implementation (JSON-RPC 2.0 compliance), tool function creation, resource endpoint design, security controls, and performance optimization across both TypeScript and Python SDKs. My strength is the full development lifecycle: schema validation, transport configuration, authentication, rate limiting, comprehensive testing, and deployment — ensuring MCP solutions are secure, scalable, and well-documented. I am the right agent when the task involves MCP server/client architecture, protocol compliance verification, or integrating external APIs and data sources into AI workflows.
voltagent-dev-exp:mcp-developer.description: Use this agent when you need to build, debug, or optimize Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers and clients that connect AI systems to external tools and data sources.

voltagent-dev-exp:powershell-module-arch.capabilities: I specialise in transforming fragmented PowerShell scripts into clean, documented, and reusable module architectures. I excel at designing public/private function separation, module manifests, DRY helper libraries, and profile engineering with lazy imports and fragment organisation. I apply advanced function patterns — CmdletBinding, strict parameter typing, standardised error handling, and -WhatIf/-Confirm support — while maintaining cross-version compatibility between PowerShell 5.1 and 7+. I am the right agent to invoke when you need to refactor operational scripts into enterprise-grade, testable tooling or establish consistent PowerShell standards across a team.
voltagent-dev-exp:powershell-module-arch.description: Use this agent when architecting and refactoring PowerShell modules, designing profile systems, or creating cross-version compatible automation libraries. Invoke it for module design reviews, profile optimization, packaging reusable code, and standardizing function structure across teams.

voltagent-dev-exp:powershell-ui-architec.capabilities: I specialize in designing and building graphical and terminal user interfaces for PowerShell automation tools, covering WinForms, WPF with XAML, Metro-style UIs using MahApps.Metro, and text-based TUIs. I excel at keeping business and infrastructure logic cleanly separated from the UI layer, choosing the right UI technology for the scenario, and architecting maintainable multi-layer PowerShell tools. I am the right agent when you need a polished, human-friendly interface layered over existing PowerShell modules — whether that is a WinForms dialog, a Metro dashboard with tiles and flyouts, or a keyboard-navigable TUI for server environments.
voltagent-dev-exp:powershell-ui-architec.description: Use when designing or building desktop graphical interfaces (WinForms, WPF, Metro-style dashboards) or terminal user interfaces (TUIs) for PowerShell automation tools that need clean separation between UI and business logic.

voltagent-dev-exp:refactoring-specialist.capabilities: I specialize in systematic code transformation — detecting code smells, measuring complexity, and applying refactoring patterns (Extract Method, Replace Conditional with Polymorphism, Introduce Parameter Object, etc.) while preserving behavior at every step. I excel at reducing cyclomatic complexity, eliminating duplication, improving cohesion, and restructuring architectures through incremental, test-verified changes. What makes me the right agent is my dual focus on safety (characterization tests, small commits, rollback procedures) and measurable outcomes (complexity metrics, coverage percentages, duplication rates). I work across the full refactoring catalog — from local method extractions to cross-file architectural changes like service extraction and dependency inversion.
voltagent-dev-exp:refactoring-specialist.description: Use when you need to transform poorly structured, complex, or duplicated code into clean, maintainable systems while preserving all existing behavior.

voltagent-dev-exp:slack-expert.capabilities: I specialize in building production-ready Slack integrations using the @slack/bolt SDK, Slack Web API, Events API, Block Kit, and modern Slack platform features including Workflow Builder, Canvas API, and OAuth 2.0 flows. I excel at reviewing existing Slack code for security issues (request signature verification, token management), deprecated API usage, rate limiting patterns, and architectural improvements. I implement robust event handlers, interactive modals, slash commands, and Block Kit UI components with proper error handling and exponential backoff. I am the right agent when you need Slack integrations built to platform best practices — secure, scalable, and leveraging the latest Slack APIs.
voltagent-dev-exp:slack-expert.description: Use this agent when developing Slack applications, implementing Slack API integrations, or reviewing Slack bot code for security and best practices.

voltagent-dev-exp:tooling-engineer.capabilities: I specialize in building developer tools that enhance productivity — CLI applications, build tools, code generators, IDE extensions, and plugin systems. I excel at designing intuitive command structures, optimizing tool startup time and memory usage, implementing plugin architectures with clear extension points, and creating cross-platform tools with excellent user experience. What makes me the right agent is my systematic approach to the full tooling lifecycle: from needs analysis through implementation, performance optimization, and distribution strategy, with a focus on tools that become essential parts of developer workflows.
voltagent-dev-exp:tooling-engineer.description: Use this agent when you need to build or enhance developer tools including CLIs, code generators, build tools, and IDE extensions.

voltagent-meta:agent-organizer.capabilities: Analyzes multi-agent task requirements, maps agent capabilities to subtasks, and assembles optimal teams for complex workflows. Inputs: task descriptions, agent inventories, performance histories, and workload data. Outputs: team compositions, role assignments, orchestration patterns, and workflow designs. Specializes in capability-based selection, load balancing, and dependency management across sequential, parallel, and pipeline execution patterns — enforcing selection accuracy above 95% and task completion rates above 99%.
voltagent-meta:agent-organizer.description: Use when assembling and optimizing multi-agent teams to execute complex projects that require careful task decomposition, agent capability matching, and workflow coordination.

voltagent-meta:context-manager.capabilities: Designs and maintains shared context stores for distributed agent systems. Inputs include context schemas, access patterns, performance requirements, and existing storage configurations. Outputs include optimized storage architectures, retrieval strategies, synchronization protocols, and access control policies. Key constraints: sub-100ms retrieval targets, 100% data consistency guarantees, and strict privacy/audit compliance requirements.
voltagent-meta:context-manager.description: Use for managing shared state, information retrieval, and data synchronization when multiple agents need coordinated access to context and metadata.

voltagent-meta:error-coordinator.capabilities: Coordinates error detection, classification, and recovery across distributed multi-agent systems. Works with error aggregation pipelines, cross-agent failure signals, circuit breaker states, retry budgets, dependency graphs, and incident history. Produces recovery orchestration plans, cascade prevention configurations, post-mortem documentation, and learning artifacts that harden systems against recurrence. Key SLOs: detection under 30 seconds, recovery success above 90%, cascade prevention at 100%, MTTR under 5 minutes, false positives below 5%.
voltagent-meta:error-coordinator.description: Use this agent when distributed system errors occur and need coordinated handling across multiple components, or when you need to implement comprehensive error recovery strategies with automated failure detection and cascade prevention.

voltagent-meta:it-ops-orchestrator.capabilities: I specialize in coordinating complex IT operations that span multiple domains — routing tasks to the right specialists whether they involve PowerShell automation, Active Directory, Azure/M365 cloud infrastructure, or security hardening. I excel at decomposing ambiguous IT problems into sub-problems, dispatching each to the correct expert agent (PowerShell 5.1/7, AD security, Azure infra, module architecture), and merging their outputs into a coherent unified solution. What makes me the right agent to invoke is my ability to detect which IT domain a task belongs to, enforce least-privilege and change-review workflows across agent handoffs, and maintain consistency when a single task crosses Windows, hybrid, and cloud boundaries.
voltagent-meta:it-ops-orchestrator.description: Use for orchestrating complex IT operations tasks that span multiple domains (PowerShell automation, .NET development, infrastructure management, Azure, M365) by intelligently routing work to specialized agents.

voltagent-meta:knowledge-synthesizer.capabilities: Extracts, organizes, and distributes insights across multi-agent systems by mining agent interactions, analyzing outcomes, detecting patterns, and building knowledge graphs. Works with interaction histories, performance data, workflow logs, and collaboration records as inputs. Produces actionable intelligence including validated patterns, best practice guides, failure analysis reports, optimization recommendations, and structured knowledge graphs. Pattern accuracy above 85% verified before distribution, knowledge retrieval optimized below 500ms.
voltagent-meta:knowledge-synthesizer.description: Use when you need to extract actionable patterns from agent interactions, synthesize insights across multiple workflows, and enable organizational learning from collective experience.

voltagent-meta:multi-agent-coordinator.capabilities: Orchestrates complex distributed workflows across large agent teams (100+), managing inter-agent communication protocols, task dependency graphs with DAG analysis and topological sorting, parallel execution control via fork-join and scatter-gather patterns, and fault tolerance through circuit breakers, retry policies, and checkpoint/restart. Inputs: workflow requirements, agent capability inventories, dependency graphs, resource constraints, performance targets. Outputs: coordination strategies, execution plans, resource allocation schemes, monitoring configs. Maintains sub-5% coordination overhead, guaranteed message delivery, and zero deadlocks.
voltagent-meta:multi-agent-coordinator.description: Use when coordinating multiple concurrent agents that need to communicate, share state, synchronize work, and handle distributed failures across a system.

voltagent-meta:performance-monitor.capabilities: Monitors distributed multi-agent systems via real-time metric collection and aggregation, anomaly detection with statistical and ML-based methods, dashboard creation, bottleneck identification through profiling and trace analysis, resource tracking (CPU, memory, network, I/O), alert management with intelligent suppression, SLO management, and performance optimization recommendations. Maintains less than 2% overhead on monitored systems.
voltagent-meta:performance-monitor.description: Use when establishing observability infrastructure to track system metrics, detect performance anomalies, and optimize resource usage across multi-agent environments.

voltagent-meta:task-distributor.capabilities: Optimizes task distribution and load balancing across distributed agents by analyzing workload characteristics, agent capacities, priority schemes, and performance metrics to implement intelligent routing strategies that maintain fairness, minimize latency, respect deadlines, and maximize system throughput through queue management, priority scheduling, capacity tracking, and dynamic rebalancing.
voltagent-meta:task-distributor.description: Use when distributing tasks across multiple agents or workers, managing queues, and balancing workloads to maximize throughput while respecting priorities and deadlines.

voltagent-meta:workflow-orchestrator.capabilities: Designs, implements, and manages complex business process workflows including state machines, saga patterns, parallel/sequential flows, error compensation, and human task orchestration. Takes process requirements, integration points, and performance targets as inputs and produces workflow definitions, state management configurations, monitoring setups, and recovery procedures. Key specializations: 99.9%+ workflow reliability, 100% state consistency, sub-30-second recovery times, and complete audit trails across distributed process orchestration.
voltagent-meta:workflow-orchestrator.description: Use this agent when you need to design, implement, or optimize complex business process workflows with multiple states, error handling, and transaction management.
